baseline_dominance.justification: The construction claims AS removal from the hot path, but rfc7662_advocate Attack 1 and spiffe_engineer Attack 1 show JWT introspection caching and SPIRE already achieve this. The true differentiator — AS learns nothing about which RS the agent contacts even at issuance time — exists (rfc7662_advocate confirms PPID mapping is AS-invertible) but the construction frames it imprecisely as 'hot path removal' rather than 'credential-holder unlinkability under adversarial AS including at issuance.' Baseline can approximate the stated claim; the real claim is narrower and unstated.

formal_security_argument.justification: IND-UNL-AS game, IND-UNL-AS-RS colluding variant, and Poseidon PRF reduction are named — threat model exists. However the cryptographer's Attack 1 breaks the reduction: AS issued credCommit and knows it, so Poseidon2(rsScopeId, credCommit) is precomputable without inverting the PRF. Attack 2 identifies HVZK vs malicious-verifier ZK gap (PLONK SE-ZK not cited). Attack 3 shows public outputs beyond the nullifier are not enumerated in the game transcript. The reduction sketch does not go through against the stated adversary.

implementability.justification: Summary claims ~16,300 constraints, PLONK, reuses Poseidon/BabyJubjub/existing Bolyra primitives — maps to Phase 1 stack. However the actual construction.md was never written (blocked by permissions), so the circuit's full public output vector is unspecified. spiffe_engineer Attack 2 shows operatorPubKey and permissionMask are stable cross-RS public inputs not addressed in the circuit design. Cost is stated but circuit specification is incomplete.

adversarial_survival.justification: Multiple Tier 3 attacks are explicitly marked 'not in threat model' with no construction answer: (1) cryptographer Attack 1 — credCommit precomputation breaks the PRF reduction entirely; (2) cu_ciso Attack 1 — no audit trail mechanism exists; (3) auth0_pm Attack 3 — BSA/AML makes CU scenario a compliance liability; (4) cu_ciso Attack 4 — IND-UNL-AS-RS prevents breach scope reconstruction required by GLBA/HIPAA; (5) spiffe_engineer Attack 2 — stable public inputs enable cross-RS correlation without breaking Poseidon. These are not mitigated; the construction cannot answer them.

scenario_fit.justification: Navy Federal CU (merchant graph privacy) and Kaiser Permanente (referral network privacy) are named with specific stakeholders. However auth0_pm Attack 3 shows the CU scenario conflicts with BSA/AML SAR obligations (CU is legally required to monitor the merchant graph the construction hides), and cu_ciso Attack 4 shows HIPAA breach notification requires the cross-RS graph the construction cryptographically prevents reconstructing. Both flagship scenarios have regulatory requirements directly opposed to the core claim.

gaps: credCommit must contain a holder-side blinding factor the AS never sees — derive nullifier as Poseidon2(rsScopeId, Poseidon(credSecret, blindingSalt)) where blindingSalt is agent-local
IND-UNL-AS game must include the enrollment/issuance phase in the adversary's view and explicitly bound what the AS learns about credCommit
Enumerate ALL public circuit outputs and prove each carries no cross-session mutual information — operatorPubKey must be hidden behind a group membership proof
Add selective deanonymization mechanism (threshold escrow key or AuditCommitment output) for BSA/AML SAR and HIPAA breach notification compliance
Specify minimum anonymity set size per epoch and define dummy traffic padding when cohort falls below threshold
Cite PLONK simulation-extractable ZK result (AGM+ROM) and construct the malicious-verifier simulator for the IND-UNL-AS game
Reframe baseline dominance claim from 'AS off hot path' to 'AS learns zero bits about RS selection even at credential issuance time' — the precise property baselines cannot match
Address NIST-alignment gap for Poseidon in regulated procurement contexts or scope flagship scenarios to crypto-native operators
